entries.title: PhD Fellow, Theoretical and Mathematical Physics (QMATH)
bullets: Supervision: Prof. Daniel Malz

entries.title: Integrated BS–MS Physics (Dual Degree)
entries.organization: Indian Institute of Science Education and Research (IISER), Mohali

entries.title: PhD Fellow, Theoretical and Mathematical Physics
bullets: Topic: Learning from Dynamics in Analog Quantum Simulators.
Developed a Hamiltonian-driven shadow tomography protocol for characterizing analog quantum simulators.
Developed calibration methods for estimating applied potentials in cold-atom lattice simulators from quench dynamics using tools from Hamiltonian learning.

entries.title: Visiting PhD Student, TCM Group
entries.organization: St John's College and Cavendish Laboratory, University of Cambridge
bullets: Topic: Quantum dynamics in dual unitary circuits.
Supervisors: Dr. Max McGinley (TCM Group, Dept. of Physics) and Prof. Bruno Bertini (University of Birmingham).
Investigated hierarchical generalizations of dual unitary quantum circuits, formulating stabilizer-based conditions using Clifford algebra.
Worked on circuit-level diagnostics of solvability via spatio-temporal correlation functions and operator space entanglement for non-Clifford circuits.

entries.title: External Master's Thesis
bullets: Title: Stabilizing Quantum Simulators of Gauge Theories against 1/f Noise. Grade: A (highest possible grade).
External supervisors: Prof. Jad C. Halimeh (MCQST, LMU Munich) and Prof. Philipp Hauke (University of Trento).
Local supervisor: Prof. Manabendra Nath Bera (IISER Mohali).
Illustrated the efficacy of linear gauge protection in stabilizing gauge invariance against 1/f-beta noise sources in quantum simulators.
Results showed immediate applicability to modern analog quantum simulators and digital NISQ devices.

entries.title: Summer Research Internship
bullets: Title: Non-Markovian dynamics in open quantum systems.
Supervision: Prof. R. Srikanth (PPISR), Dr. Vinayak Jagdish (University of Krakow, Poland).
Captured non-Markovianity in a quantum channel from mixing Pauli semigroups at the level of intermediate dynamical maps, via quantum process tomography on IBM's quantum simulators.

entries.title: Research Intern
bullets: Title: Quantum random number generation and quantum key distribution.
Supervision: Animesh Aaryan (CEO, TAQBIT Labs).
Simulated spontaneous parametric down-conversion in photons to generate quantum entanglement.
Worked on experimental protocols to extract cryptographically secure random numbers using a spatially entangled source quantum random number generator.
Characterized electro-optic modulators to realize differential phase shift quantum key distribution.

entries.title: Travel Grant
entries.organization: Department of Mathematical Sciences, University of Copenhagen
bullets: For a summer research stay at the Cavendish Laboratory, University of Cambridge (€4,500).
